canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, text embedding is consistently defined as the process of mapping or producing dense vector representations from text inputs. The purpose of these vectors is described as capturing the "semantic meaning of text" for use in downstream methods, with one definition specifically highlighting similarity-based applications. One paper notes that these vector representations "serve as the foundation for many natural language processing (NLP) tasks." Large language models are explicitly mentioned as being explored as "powerful text embedders." A specific operational detail mentioned in one source is that models can "take text inputs and output 3072-dimensional vectors." To evaluate this behavior, the provided papers use the MTEB benchmark.
papers: CoEvo: Coevolution ofLLMand Retrieval Model for Domain-Specific Information Retrieval
DIDS: Domain Impact-aware Data Sampling for Large Language Model Training

definitions.text: Mapping text inputs to dense vector representations for similarity-based use in downstream methods.
definitions.paper: Weighted Multi-Prompt Learning with Description-free Large Language Model Distillation

relationships.paper: Layer by Layer: Uncovering Hidden Representations in Language Models

icml25: Layer by Layer: Uncovering Hidden Representations in Language Models
What Limits Bidirectional Model’s Generative Capabilities? A Uni-Bi-Directional Mixture-of-Expert Method For Bidirectional Fine-tuning
